name: insert_propagates_to_interp_aux
code:
proof fn phi_2_insert_propagates_to_interp_aux(d1: Directory, d2: Directory, idx: nat, vaddr: nat, pte: PTE)
    requires
        d1.inv(),
        d2.inv(),
        idx < d1.entries.len(),
        d1.arch == d2.arch,
        d1.layer == d2.layer,
        d1.base_vaddr == d2.base_vaddr,
        d2.entries[idx as int].interp(d1.entry_base(idx))
            == d1.entries[idx as int].interp(d1.entry_base(idx)).insert(vaddr, pte),
        forall|j: nat| j < d1.entries.len() && j != idx
            ==> (#[trigger] d1.entries[j as int]).interp(d1.entry_base(j))
                        == d2.entries[j as int].interp(d1.entry_base(j)),
    ensures
        d2.interp_aux(0) === d1.interp_aux(0).insert(vaddr, pte),
{
    d1.lemma_entries_interp_insert_implies_interp_aux_insert(d2, idx, vaddr, pte, 0);
}
reason: Single-entry insertion propagates to interp_aux — the verified proof depends on the external_body disjointness lemma for the `idx > i` case

name: disjoint_symmetric
reason: Symmetric direction of disjointness — also depends on the external_body axiom

name: page_entry_singleton_key
code:
proof fn phi_4_page_entry_singleton_key(d: Directory, i: nat)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        i < d.entries.len(),
        d.entries[i as int] is Page,
    ensures
        d.interp_of_entry(i).contains_key(d.entry_base(i)),
        d.interp_of_entry(i).dom().len() == 1,
{
}
reason: A page entry's interpretation contains exactly one key at its entry_base — tests the singleton map structure

name: disjoint_page_entries_distinct_bases
code:
proof fn phi_5_disjoint_page_entries_distinct_bases(d: Directory, i: nat, j: nat)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        i < j,
        j < d.entries.len(),
        d.entries[i as int] is Page,
        d.entries[j as int] is Page,
    ensures
        d.entry_base(i) != d.entry_base(j),
{
    d.lemma_interp_of_entry_disjoint_mappings(i, j);
}
reason: Two page entries at different indices must have different entry_bases — consequence of the external_body disjointness, but the base distinctness should follow from arithmetic alone
